name: Band Overhead Triceps Extension
resistance_profile: The overhead arm position holds the long head under stretch throughout the range while band tension peaks as the elbows lock out overhead. Biases the long head.
cues: Keep the upper arms vertical and by the ears
Control the stretch behind the head
execution_steps: Anchor a band low (under both feet or at a low rack pin) and grip the free end(s) with both hands.
Raise the hands overhead so the upper arms sit alongside the ears, elbows bent and forearms behind the head.
Extend the elbows to press the band straight overhead until the arms lock out.
Lower under control back behind the head, keeping the upper arms vertical and still.
good_when: You want long-head emphasis with no free weights
Training at home or on the road
Adding a shoulder-flexed extension variation to your triceps work
bad_when: You want the heaviest resistance in the stretched (bottom) position
common_errors: Letting the elbows flare out wide
Dropping the upper arms forward as you fatigue
Overarching the lower back to press up
progressions: Add a pause at the deep stretch
regressions: Reduce range of motion at the bottom
